_meta.author: Peter Higgins
_meta.purpose: Machine-readable entry point for all systems interacting with the Hˢ repository. Read this file first. It provides: identity, navigation, protocol, governance, and authority resolution.
_meta.read_order: This file → HS_ADMIN.json → experiment results → reference standards → archive
_meta.authority: When this file disagrees with any other file, this file wins on structure and navigation. When experimental results disagree with reference standards, the reference standards win until regression testing validates the new results across the full experiment chain.

identity.name: Higgins Decomposition
identity.one_sentence: A deterministic compositional knowledge engine that reads, fingerprints, and catalogs the geometric identity of any system on the simplex — accumulating structural knowledge with full audit trail and traceability.
identity.operating_principle: Compositional Knowledge Engine — every pipeline run produces a diagnosis, a fingerprint, and an audit trail. Each run enriches a searchable catalog. The tool accumulates structural knowledge: past analyses become reference points for future discoveries. The tool makes tools.
identity.decomposition: Hˢ = R ∘ M ∘ E ∘ C ∘ T ∘ V ∘ S

operators.V: Variance trajectory — cumulative Aitchison variance as shape
operators.E: Entropy test — EITT geometric-mean decimation (novel / analytically bounded — O-1 resolved April 28, 2026)
operators.M: Mode synthesis — conjunction of diagnostic predicates

identity.axiom: ∀𝒳 : Hˢ(𝒳) = Hˢ(𝒳) — determinism as generating principle
identity.closing_statement: The instrument reads. The expert decides. The loop stays open.
identity.scale_range: 10⁻¹⁸ m to 10²⁶ m (44 orders of magnitude)
identity.pipeline_version: 2.0 Extended + Amalgamation + Matrix + Tensor + EDI Control Gate + CNT
identity.hci_dcdi: Dominant Carrier Displacement Index — formal name for Helmsman
identity.hci_stage1_standard_note: Higgins tensor data field layout v1.0. Fixed-scale matplotlib plate generator — 2x3 grid (Info|Legend|XZ / XY|YZ). Field-deployable, no AI dependency. Pre-scans dataset for global axis limits. Stage 1 output system CONCLUDED.

navigation._note: All paths are relative to repository root.

pipeline.entry_point: from higgins_decomposition_12step import HigginsDecomposition; hd = HigginsDecomposition(id, name, domain, carriers); hd.load_data(matrix); result = hd.run_full_extended()

experiments.range: Hs-01 through Hs-25 + Hs-LAB01 + Hs-M01 + Hs-M02 + Hs-MC4 + Hs-STD = 30 experiment folders

interactive_tools.note: All interactive tools in one folder for fast demo access. CoDaWork demos are copies from papers/codawork2026/.

standards_edition.description: Self-contained Jupyter notebook — 18 cells, auto-installs dependencies, auto-fetches pipeline from GitHub, 3 built-in reference standards, runs all advanced analyses (amalgamation, power map, fingerprint). Conference handout tool.

m_series_tools.note: M-series HTML tools live in their experiment folders (not tools/interactive/) as they are experiment-specific baseline references.

visualization_standard.description: The Manifold Projector is the mandatory terminal visualization for every Hˢ analysis. It renders the compositional data manifold as a 3D interactive radar-polygon tube — each time step is a radar chart fingerprint (per-carrier CLR values normalized to 0-1) stacked along the Z-axis with perspective projection (FOV=700). The slideshow leads to this display. This IS the diagram of the system.
features: per-run min-max CLR normalization (each run normalized independently to preserve shape fidelity)
perturbation mapping (|ΔCLR| as outward spikes, green→yellow→red)
trails, ghost, labels, time slider, view presets
visualization_standard.projection_fidelity: A data straight line on the simplex projects as a smooth monotonic radar polygon deformation — no false curvature because normalization is per-run min-max and linear. Per-run normalization ensures each system's shape is faithfully rendered without cross-run scale contamination.
visualization_standard.normalization_rule: Per-run min-max. Each run's CLR values are normalized to [0,1] using that run's own min and max. This preserves the geometric identity of each system. Global normalization across runs is prohibited — it distorts shape fidelity. Validated by MC-4 (5/5 PASS) and STD (3/3 PASS) calibration series.

boundary_markers.degenerate: DEGEN diamond marker at center axis when CLR spread < 1e-4. No polygon drawn.
boundary_markers.missing: Red X marker at vertex when carrier CLR < -10. Polygon drawn with valid carriers only.
boundary_markers.lock_acquisition: Green dashed ring with offset value. First valid timestep after DEGEN. Documents instrument resolving boundary.
boundary_markers.lock_loss: Amber dashed ring with offset value. Last valid timestep before DEGEN. Symmetric with acquisition.

visualization_standard.pipeline_rule: Every experiment MUST generate a Manifold Projector HTML as its final visual output. The pipeline slideshow is the journey; the projector is the destination.
visualization_standard.usage: Open hs_projector.html in a browser, drop any pipeline results JSON or projector manifest. The universal instrument replaces the need to generate per-experiment projector HTML files.

maintenance.description: Self-updating maintenance procedure — checklist for repo upkeep after any structural change

onboarding_sequence: 5. Run a reference standard to verify the pipeline works
6. Compare your DUT results against the reference library

terminology_gate.rule: Check canonical_names and communication_standards before generating any text. If a term appears in a NOT list, do not use it.

claim_tiers: core_science — primary Hˢ scientific content (proven or strongly evidenced)
validated_companion — strong secondary instrument or descriptor
engineering_control — integrity, release, regression, operating-envelope layer
conference_safe — suitable for CoDa/math audience with minimal editing
point_of_interest — noteworthy finding, not yet promoted to core
archived — superseded, retired, or historical (preserved, not active)

feed_forward_model.P_proportional: Mainstream pipeline and active experiments. Responds to current findings.
feed_forward_model.I_integral: Archive of all past experiments, methods, and decisions. JSON snapshots. Never deleted. Provides long-term stability.
feed_forward_model.D_derivative: Waypoint system in EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md. Declares position, target, gate, drift boundary.
feed_forward_model.FF_feed_forward: New information is forward-biased. Diagnosed for current use. Historical data checked but not privileged.

instrument_certification.status: CERTIFIED — MC-4 calibration passed (5/5 test objects), CoDaWork-certifiable methodology chain, DUT posture active

three_laws.first: The instrument does not fabricate. If the shape does not match expectation, the data contains that structure.
three_laws.second: Every nibble is preserved. No data point is discarded, smoothed, or suppressed. Degenerate and missing markers tag all boundary conditions.
three_laws.third: Confidence is stated. The methodology chain (closure, CLR, spline, normalization, projection) is certifiable to CoDaWork standards. No known misstep exists.

instrument_certification.dut_principle: The data is always the Device Under Test. The projector is never the DUT. An unexpected shape is a finding about the data, not a defect of the instrument. The anomaly is the interesting part — it must never be lost.
instrument_certification.confidence_statement: The methodology chain is sound, the instrument is calibrated, and the projection is a faithful geometric rendering of the compositional structure within the data.

authority_stack: 0. HS_DATA_INTEGRITY_LAW.md — three laws of data integrity, instrument certification, DUT principle (governing)
3. Reference standard library — calibration values and thresholds
4. Accumulated experiment results — validated chain of evidence
5. Individual experiment results — newest, lowest authority until regression-tested
6. Archive — historical context, not active authority

conflict_resolution.rule: When sources disagree, higher-numbered authority yields to lower-numbered. Exception: a consistent pattern of new results that survives full regression testing (HIVP chain) can update any authority level. The pattern must be documented in EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md before the update takes effect.
conflict_resolution.example: If a new experiment contradicts the reference standards, the reference standards win until the new result is replicated, regression-tested, and documented. If the new result survives, the reference standards are updated and the old values are archived.

acceptance_criteria: Pass all 4 input guards (D≥2, N≥5, no NaN/Inf, scale sanity)
Produce a valid extended pipeline run (no crashes, all codes emitted)
Document with full diagnostic codes and claim tier assignment
Compare against reference standard library (position relative to ceiling, floor, physical standard)
Survive regression against existing experiments (HIVP chain principle)
Meet communication standards (terminology, tone, language, content labels)

archive_protocol.trigger: Content is archived when superseded, retired, or no longer actively referenced.
archive_protocol.format: JSON snapshot with timestamp, reason, and pointer to replacement.
archive_protocol.retention: Permanent. Archive is the integral term — it provides stability.
archive_protocol.refresh: Full archive review included in every major waypoint.

audit_system.principle: Every pipeline operation produces an audit record. Every record captures: what ran, when, input hash, output hash, duration, status, and breakpoint decisions. The chain hash provides tamper-evident integrity across the full trail.

presets.cautious: HOLD on FLAG, errors, and guard failures
presets.strict: ABORT on errors and guard failures, HOLD on FLAG
presets.audit: LOG_ONLY everywhere — maximum traceability, no stops
presets.development: HOLD at every step — step-through debugging

breakpoints.safety_rule: Every loop (catalog batch, regression suite, cross-check) has an explicit break condition at BP-LOOP-ITER. No infinite loops are possible — the audit system enforces termination.

audit_system.traceability: Full chain of custody from raw data to final diagnosis. Each operation records input/output hashes. The chain hash is a SHA-256 of the sequential operation record — modifying any record invalidates the chain. ISO 17025-compatible.

controller_architecture.principle: Hˢ operates as an industrial compositional analysis component. The controller wraps the pipeline in a state machine with event-driven integration. Hˢ-GOV provides top-level supervisory governance.

controller.event_bus: Publish-subscribe pattern for external system integration. Events: state_change, classification, fingerprint, hold, error, flag.

controller_architecture.verified: Multi-controller supervised run passed — 2 controllers, both completed, fingerprints registered, audit chains valid.

knowledge_engine.principle: Hˢ is a compositional knowledge engine. Every run produces: (1) a diagnosis (codes + structural modes), (2) a fingerprint (searchable geometric identity), (3) an audit trail (chain of custody). Each run enriches the catalog. Past analyses become reference points for future discoveries.
knowledge_engine.accumulation_cycle: run → diagnose → fingerprint → catalog → compare → discover → run
knowledge_engine.loop_safety: The accumulation cycle is open-ended but never closed-loop. Every catalog batch has BP-LOOP-ITER between iterations and BP-LOOP-END at termination. Every loop has a maximum iteration count or explicit termination condition. The audit trail records every iteration with input/output hashes for post-hoc verification.

tools_generated.regression_suite: hs_testgen.py --regression — generates self-contained regression test from catalog

waypoint_protocol.requirement: Every waypoint in EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md must declare:
fields: next_waypoint — what the next target is (future)
gate_conditions — what must be true to advance
drift_boundary — what would constitute deviation from trajectory

api._note: Minimal programmatic interface for any system to use Hˢ.

run_experiment.codes: from hs_codes import generate_codes; codes = generate_codes(result)
run_experiment.report: from hs_reporter import report; text = report(codes, lang='en')
run_experiment.report_all: from hs_reporter import report_all_languages; all_reports = report_all_languages(codes)
run_experiment.metrology: from hs_metrology import run_full_metrology; report = run_full_metrology()
run_experiment.controller_single: from hs_controller import HsController; ctrl = HsController('ID','Name','DOMAIN',carriers); ctrl.start(data, preset='cautious'); result = ctrl.get_result()
run_experiment.controller_governed: from hs_controller import HufGov; gov = HufGov(mode='SUPERVISED'); cid = gov.register_controller(ctrl); gov.run_controller(cid, data); gov.save_state('gov_state.json')
run_experiment.controller_inspect: ctrl.inspect()  # returns state, audit summary, breakpoint status
run_experiment.power_map_from_data: from hs_sensitivity import ComponentPowerMapper; mapper = ComponentPowerMapper(data, carriers, domain='DOMAIN'); power_map = mapper.run_full_analysis()
run_experiment.amalgamation_run: from hs_amalgamation import AmalgamationEngine; engine = AmalgamationEngine(data, carriers); results = engine.run_all_levels()

quick_test.description: Verify pipeline is working by running a reference standard.
quick_test.code: import numpy as np; data = np.array([[0.5,0.3,0.2],[0.4,0.4,0.2],[0.3,0.5,0.2],[0.45,0.35,0.2],[0.35,0.45,0.2]]); hd = HigginsDecomposition('TEST','Quick Test','TEST',carriers=['A','B','C']); hd.load_data(data); r = hd.run_full_extended(); print(r['extended']['universal']['claim_status'])

theoretical_extensions._note: Extensions beyond the core pipeline, documented in HUF/science/ and Hs EXECUTIVE_SUMMARY.md.

o1_resolution.statement: EITT residual is analytically bounded by closed-form Hessian trace bound.
o1_resolution.resolved_by: Gemini via CLR Taylor expansion proof
o1_resolution.collective: Peter Higgins + Grok + Copilot + ChatGPT + Gemini + Claude

dimensional_collapse.statement: Components below the compositional horizon (min(x_i) ≈ 0.05) undergo dimensional collapse. Survivors reconstitute on a lower-dimensional simplex with higher coherence.
cycle: Reconstitution at D-k with new interior stability

compositional_horizon.status: Empirical (formal derivation is Open Problem D-6 / O-4)
compositional_horizon.statement: Below min(x_i) ≈ 0.05, components are causally disconnected from the analysis. Curvature artefacts dominate signal.

pruning_process.statement: Geometric-mean decimation is a smooth pruning process: preserves robust interior structure, dissolves fragile boundary structure.

tensor_functor.statement: Hˢ formulated as a five-layer tensor functor: Hˢ = ρ ∘ Tr ∘ Σ ∘ Λ ∘ S with explicit rank changes (1,1)→(1,1)→(1,1)→(0,2)→(0,0)→label.
tensor_functor.naturality: CLR ≡ ILR (orthonormal Helmert). ALR breaks naturality.

matrix_diagnostics.statement: 24 MX-* diagnostic codes from V(t) covariance matrix eigendecomposition. 4 new structural modes (SM-FRZ, SM-THR, SM-MXR, SM-EDP). Pipeline Step 6.5.

impedance_match_observation.statement: Low reflection coefficient Γ = √(1 − λ₁/Tr(V)) is a measured consequence of rank-1 dominant covariance across tested systems.
impedance_match_observation.note: Previously labelled 'Balun Theorem'. Renamed to 'Impedance Match Observation' per science-neutral stance.

poincare_disc_connection.statement: Circularity ratio ρ = r_min/r_max mapped to Poincaré disc via Cayley transform. 9 PD-* diagnostic codes for hyperbolic structure analysis.

higgins_coordinate_system.statement: Complete coordinate system on the simplex with Euclidean, polar, hyperbolic, and projective representations. Handbook v4.2.

edi_control_gate.statement: Eigenstructure Distortion Index as internal engine control (Step 5.5). EDI = √(angular² + spectral²)/√2. Validates CLR transform necessity.

compositional_navigation_tensor.statement: CNT(x) = (θ, ω, κ, σ) — bearing, angular velocity, steering sensitivity, helmsman from any closed composition via CLR polar decomposition.

components.theta: Bearing tensor — atan2(h_j, h_i) for all D(D-1)/2 pairs
components.omega: Angular velocity — arccos of normalised CLR dot product

findings: Steering sensitivity divergence at simplex boundary

codawork_posture.previous: Scientific Surrender — presenting an empirical mystery
codawork_posture.current: Calibration Proof — presenting a fully bounded, analytically governed instrument
codawork_posture.trigger: O-1 resolution neutralizes the Jensen's inequality attack

lineage.engineering_ancestry: Rogue Wave Audio DADC-DADI-ADAC loudspeaker diffraction correction chain (2024-2025)
lineage.mathematical_foundation: Aitchison (1982/1986) simplex geometry, Shannon (1948) entropy, Varley (2025) information theory for complex systems
lineage.separation_reason: Hˢ matured into a standalone mathematical instrument. Hˢ-GOV provides governance. The parent HUF repository remains as the historical development sibling.
